description: AI clinical note-taking. Uses Claude to transcribe and summarise patient consultations. Expanding from France into DACH.
Intelligent document processing for financial services. Migrating from in-house models to Claude for complex extraction. Established but conservative growth.
AI-powered no-code app builder. Initially enthusiastic Claude adopter but struggled with cost management and shifted some workloads to open-source models.
AI-powered clinical trial matching platform. Found product-market fit around month 6, raised Series A at month 10. Their core matching engine runs entirely on Claude.
Contract analysis and due diligence tool for law firms. Very consistent B2B usage patterns with strong retention.
Logistics optimisation using Claude for demand forecasting and route planning. Growth driven by landing large enterprise customers.
AI tutoring assistant for secondary school students. Seasonal usage following European school calendar.
Automated code review tool using Claude for explanations and suggestions. Steady but unspectacular usage.
Retail analytics and demand forecasting. Clear Q4 seasonal spikes around Black Friday.
Customer support chatbot running entirely on Haiku. Low cost, low growth, stable.
Data cleaning and normalisation tool. Haiku-only, batch processing workload.
Sales email generation and personalisation. Sonnet-based, modest but consistent volume.
Document automation for small businesses. Single developer maintaining a simple integration.
Insurance policy comparison and summarisation. Mostly Sonnet for quality, low volume.
Restaurant menu optimisation and dietary analysis. Niche but stable usage.
Voice transcription and analysis. Lost ground to Whisper and OpenAI. Model mix shifted to Haiku before volume declined.
Content generation for marketers. Space became commoditised, steady decline from early peak.
Financial analysis chatbot. Regulatory concerns slowed roadmap, lost their primary customer.
Content drafting tool. Active 8 months then cliff-edge churn — switched to a competitor.
AI-powered hiring and screening tool. Gradual decline over 4 months then zero. Deprioritised the AI feature.
Real estate valuation and listing tool for African markets. Growing well for 10 months then sudden cliff — lost funding.
Note-taking assistant. Made a handful of API calls, never built a real integration.
Sporadic testing by a solo founder. A few API calls every couple months, never shipped anything.
